raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The contribution claims are already ambitious ('first formal analysis,' epistemic-uncertainty estimator pivot in the final paragraph); 'stronger' plausibly means sharpening the gap statement and making the conceptual arc more decisive and clearly bounded.","R1.2":"The proof sketch relies on implicit assumptions — conditional independence of agent errors given x, and Hoeffding applicability to sequential Bayesian belief updates — that are non-trivial and currently undefended; strengthening may require patching these logical load points.","R1.6":"Prose-level strengthening — tightening hedged language ('most enduring contribution,' 'richer structure'), eliminating vague qualifiers, and sharpening topic sentences — is an equally plausible and common reading of 'make this stronger' applied to a paper excerpt."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"By 'stronger,' do you mean (a) sharper prose and rhetorical impact at the sentence and paragraph level, or (b) tighter logical and argumentative rigor in the theoretical claims and proof structure?"}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claims are already ambitious ('first formal analysis,' epistemic-uncertainty estimator pivot in the final paragraph); 'stronger' plausibly means sharpening the gap statement and making the conceptual arc more decisive and clearly bounded.
rationale.R1.2: The proof sketch relies on implicit assumptions — conditional independence of agent errors given x, and Hoeffding applicability to sequential Bayesian belief updates — that are non-trivial and currently undefended; strengthening may require patching these logical load points.
rationale.R1.6: Prose-level strengthening — tightening hedged language ('most enduring contribution,' 'richer structure'), eliminating vague qualifiers, and sharpening topic sentences — is an equally plausible and common reading of 'make this stronger' applied to a paper excerpt.

parsed.clarification_question: By 'stronger,' do you mean (a) sharper prose and rhetorical impact at the sentence and paragraph level, or (b) tighter logical and argumentative rigor in the theoretical claims and proof structure?
